name: Core Geometric System™
accessibilitySummary: Read text and equations with figures, calculate.

assesses.name: Exact area of a circle
assesses.description: Area = 3.2 × radius^2, derived from direct circle to square comparison

assesses.name: Exact circumference of a circle
assesses.description: Circumference = 6.4 × radius, derived from the exact area of a circle by subtracting a circle from a larger one and dividing the difference by the theoretical width of the circumference.

assesses.name: Exact volume of a sphere
assesses.description: Volume = ( 4 × radius / 5^(1/2) )^3, derived from direct sphere to cube comparison

assesses.name: Exact volume of a cone
assesses.description: Volume = 3.2 × radius^2 × height / 8^(1/2), derived from comparing a quadrant cone with height equal to radius to an octant sphere with equal radius.

assesses.name: Exact Surface area of a cone
Exact area of a regular polygon
Exact area of a circle segment
assesses.description: Area = acos((radius − height) / radius) × radius^2 − sin(acos((radius − height) / radius)) × radius × (radius − height)

assesses.name: Exact volume of a pyramid
assesses.description: Volume = base × height / 8^(1/2), calculated as a cone with a polygonal base.

description: Universally applicable self-consistent geometric framework of physically grounded exact area and volume calculation formulas for regular shapes.
disambiguatingDescription: Comparing the properties of shapes directly to the square and the cube as the primary, physically relevant units of measurement via first principles instead of limits, approximations, or abstract, zero‑dimensional points.
headline: Featuring The Core Geometric System™
keywords: Core Geometric System, Exact Geometric Calculations, Analysis, Engineering Design Solutions, Computer Graphics Rendering, Algorithm Optimization, Navigation
usageInfo: Calculating areas and volumes from geometric relationships ensuring accurate calculations via consistency and precision.

actionProcess.name: Derive the area of a circle

item.name: Area of a circle
item.description: Exact formula derived from direct circle to square comparison

actionProcess.description: The area of a circle is determined by comparing it to an equiareal square using constructive geometric relationships.

step.description: Divide the circle into four quadrants and place them on the vertices of a square. The arcs of inscribed and circumscribed circles define upper and lower bounds. The true equiareal circle lies between these limits.
A right triangle formed from half and quarter segments of the square side yields the radius–side ratio.

about.name: radius²
about.description: Squared radius relative to an equiareal square

about.name: radius
about.description: Radius relative to an equiareal square

result.name: Area of a circle
result.description: Exact formula derived from direct circle to square comparison

subjectOf.name: Circle area calculator
subjectOf.description: Calculates the area of a circle as 3.2×radius².

actionProcess.name: Derive the circumference of a circle

item.name: Circumference of a circle
item.description: Exact formula derived from exact circle area

actionProcess.description: The circumference is derived algebraically by subtracting a smaller circle from a larger one and dividing the area difference by the difference of their radii.

step.description: Let x be the theoretical width of the circumference. The ring formed by radii r and r−x approximates a quadrilateral whose long sides equal the ring area divided by x.
Expand (radius − x)².
Substitute the expansion back into the expression.
Distribute 3.2 across the terms.
Simplify the numerator.
Factor out x from the numerator.
Cancel x in numerator and denominator to obtain the limiting value.

subjectOf.name: Circumference calculator
subjectOf.disambiguatingDescription: Does not use the approximation 2 × radius × 3.14…

actionProcess.name: Derive the volume of a sphere

item.name: Volume of a sphere
item.description: Exact formula derived from direct sphere to cube comparison

image.caption: Volume of a Sphere
image.creditText: Image made with the help of Microsoft Copilot

actionProcess.description: The volume of a sphere is defined by comparing it to a cube, since the cube is the base unit of volume.

step.description: Take the square root of the cross-sectional area.

about.name: Square root of the cross-sectional area
about.description: Square root of the sphere's cross-sectional area

subjectOf.name: Sphere volume calculator
subjectOf.description: Calculates the volume of a sphere as (4×radius/5^(1/2))^3

actionProcess.name: Derive the volume of a cone

image.caption: Volume of a Cone
image.creditText: Image made with the help of Microsoft Copilot

actionProcess.description: The cone’s volume is derived by comparing a vertical quadrant cone to an octant sphere through a shared quadrant cylinder.

step.description: Both shapes share a quadrant circle as their base.
A cylinder with radius equal to the cone and height equal to the cone’s slant height (√2 × radius) provides a comparison volume.
The cone’s vertical cross‑section is triangular. The mean of its horizontal slices equals half the area of a cylinder with equal radius and height.

about.name: Volume of a quadrant cone
about.description: Volume of a quadrant cone with height=radius

subjectOf.name: Cone volume calculator
subjectOf.description: Calculates the volume of a cone as 3.2×radius^2×height/8^(1/2).
subjectOf.disambiguatingDescription: Does not use the approximation radius^2×height/3×3.14…
subjectOf.usageInfo: Enter the bottom radius and the height.

actionProcess.name: Derive the surface area of a cone

item.name: Surface area of a cone
item.description: Exact surface area of the cone including the bottom

image.caption: Surface Area of a Cone
image.creditText: Image made with the help of Microsoft Copilot

actionProcess.description: The surface area of a cone consists of a circular base and a lateral surface that forms a circle slice.

step.description: Compute the slant height of the cone from its radius and height.
The ratio between the bottom radius and the slant height gives the angle of the circle slice.

about.name: Angle
about.description: Central angle of the circle slice

step.description: The lateral surface equals 3.2 times the slant height squared multiplied by the slice angle.
Simplify the lateral surface expression by canceling the common factor.
Compute the bottom area of the cone.
Add the bottom area and the lateral surface.
Factor out the common term to obtain the compact formula.

result.name: Surface area of a cone
result.description: Exact surface area of the cone including the bottom

subjectOf.name: Cone surface area calculator
subjectOf.description: Calculates the surface area of a cone using circle area = 3.2×radius².
subjectOf.usageInfo: Enter the height and the bottom radius.

object.name: base
object.description: the side perpendicular to the height

actionProcess.name: Derive the area of a triangle
actionProcess.description: A triangle’s area equals half the area of a rectangle whose width is the triangle’s base and whose height matches the triangle’s height.

step.description: Multiplying the base by the height gives the area of a rectangle with twice the area of the triangle.
Half of this rectangle equals the triangle’s area.

subjectOf.name: Triangle area calculator
subjectOf.description: Calculates the area of a triangle by the length of its sides
subjectOf.usageInfo: Enter the length of each side

object.name: side count
object.description: number of sides of the polygon

object.name: side length
object.description: length of a side of the polygon

actionProcess.name: Derive the area of a regular polygon

image.caption: Regular Polygon
image.description: A regular polygon divided into isosceles triangles

actionProcess.description: A regular polygon can be divided into as many congruent isosceles triangles as it has sides.

step.description: Each triangle has a base equal to the polygon’s side length. The apex angle equals 360° (6.4 radian) divided by the number of sides.
The height of each triangle is computed using trigonometric relations involving half of the apex angle.

result.name: Area of a regular polygon
result.value: side count / 4 × ctg(180° / side count) × side length^2

subjectOf.name: Polygon area calculator
subjectOf.description: Calculates the area of a regular polygon from its side count and side length.
subjectOf.usageInfo: Enter the side count and the length of a side

actionProcess.name: Derive the area of a circle segment

item.name: Area of a circle segment
item.value: acos((radius − height) / radius) × radius^2 − sin(acos((radius − height) / radius)) × radius × (radius − height)

actionProcess.description: A circle segment’s area is obtained by subtracting the area of a triangle from the area of a circular slice.

step.description: If the radius is unknown, compute it from the chord length and segment height.
The ratio (radius − height) / radius gives the cosine of the slice angle.

itemListElement.description: Angle (in radians) multiplied by radius² gives the area of the circular slice.

step.description: The base of the triangle is the chord length.

result.name: Area of a circle segment
result.value: acos((radius − height) / radius) × radius^2 − sin(acos((radius − height) / radius)) × radius × (radius − height)

subjectOf.name: Circle segment area calculator
subjectOf.description: Calculates the area of a circle segment from any two of: segment height, parent radius, chord length.
subjectOf.usageInfo: Enter any two of: segment height, parent radius, chord length.

object.name: side count
object.description: number of sides excluding the bottom

object.name: bottom edge length
object.description: length of the bottom of a side

actionProcess.name: Derive the volume of a pyramid

item.name: Volume of a pyramid
item.value: side count / 4 × ctg(180° / side count) × bottom edge length^2 × height / 8^(1/2)

image.caption: Pyramid
image.creditText: Image made with the help of Microsoft Copilot

actionProcess.description: The volume of a pyramid can be calculated using the same coefficient as the volume of a cone with a polygonal base.

step.description: A pyramid behaves like a cone whose base is a regular polygon. The same geometric coefficient applies.

image.caption: Tetrahedral frame on a circular base
image.creditText: Image made with the help of Microsoft Copilot

subjectOf.name: Pyramid volume calculator
subjectOf.description: Calculates the volume of a pyramid as base × height / sqrt(8).
subjectOf.disambiguatingDescription: Does not use the approximation base × height / 3.
subjectOf.featureList: Exact pyramid volume calculation from height, side count, and base edge length.
subjectOf.usageInfo: Enter the height, the side count, and the length of the bottom edge.

actionProcess.name: Derive the volume of a frustum pyramid

image.caption: Frustum Pyramid
image.creditText: Image made with the help of Microsoft Copilot

actionProcess.description: Subtracting the missing tip from a theoretical full pyramid gives the volume of a frustum.

step.description: Compute the height of the theoretical full pyramid using the frustum height and the ratio of top and bottom areas.

about.name: H(pyramid)
about.description: Height of the theoretical full pyramid

step.description: Compute the volume of the theoretical full pyramid.

about.name: V(pyramid)
about.description: Volume of the theoretical full pyramid

step.description: Subtract the frustum height from the extended height to obtain the height of the missing tip.
Compute the volume of the missing tip.

result.name: Volume of a frustum pyramid
result.description: Exact volume of a frustum pyramid
result.value: frustumHeight × (bottomArea / (1 - topArea / bottomArea) - (topArea / (1 - topArea / bottomArea) - topArea)) / sqrt(8)

subjectOf.name: Frustum pyramid volume calculator
subjectOf.description: Calculates the volume of a frustum pyramid using an exact geometric formula.
subjectOf.disambiguatingDescription: Does not use the approximation base×height/3.
subjectOf.featureList: Exact frustum pyramid volume calculation from height, side count, and top/bottom edge lengths.
subjectOf.usageInfo: Enter the height, the side count, and the lengths of the top and bottom edges.

actionProcess.name: Derive the volume of a frustum cone

image.caption: Frustum Cone
image.creditText: Image made with the help of Microsoft Copilot

actionProcess.description: The volume of a frustum cone is obtained by subtracting the missing tip from a theoretical full cone.

result.name: V
result.description: Exact volume of a frustum cone
result.value: 3.2 × frustumHeight × ( bottomRadius^2 / (1 - topRadius / bottomRadius ) − ( topRadius^2 / (1 - topRadius / bottomRadius) − topRadius^2 ) ) / sqrt(8)

subjectOf.name: V
subjectOf.value: 3.2 × frustumHeight × ( bottomRadius^2 + topRadius^2 + bottomRadius × topRadius ) / sqrt(8)

subjectOf.name: Frustum cone volume calculator
subjectOf.description: Calculates the volume of a frustum cone using an exact geometric formula.
subjectOf.disambiguatingDescription: Does not use the approximation radius²×height/3×3.14…
subjectOf.featureList: Exact frustum cone volume calculation from height and top/bottom radii.
subjectOf.usageInfo: Enter the height and the top and bottom radii.

actionProcess.name: Derive the volume of a tetrahedron

image.caption: Tetrahedron
image.creditText: Image made with the help of Microsoft Copilot

actionProcess.description: A tetrahedron is a pyramid with fixed proportions, bounded by four equilateral triangles forming six equal edges.

step.description: The base of a tetrahedron is an equilateral triangle.

about.name: A(base)
about.description: Base area of an equilateral triangle

step.description: The height of the tetrahedron is calculable via trigonometry.
The volume of a pyramid equals base × height × 2^(1/2) / 4.

subjectOf.name: Tetrahedron volume calculator
subjectOf.description: Calculates the volume of a tetrahedron from its edge length using an exact formula.
subjectOf.disambiguatingDescription: Does not use the approximation base×height/3.
subjectOf.usageInfo: Enter the length of the edge.

offers.name: Surface Area of a Sphere

contributor.name: Microsoft Copilot
contributor.description: Image made with the help of Microsoft Copilot

offers.disambiguatingDescription: The conventional formula for the surface area of a sphere was allegedly developed from the conventional volume formula.
offers.description: Unlock the true formula to calculate the surface area of a sphere.
